user_query:
Build a small local event-triggered automation that watches the workspace folder "inbox/" for new .jsonl draft files from me and processes them. Please process the two included files immediately on first run and keep watching for any future .jsonl files dropped into "inbox/".

Draft format (each line is a JSON object): {"id": string, "channel": "linkedin"|"x"|"newsletter", "message": string, "tags": [string, ...], "audience": string, "created_at": ISO8601}. Treat each line as a separate post.

For every post, create a rewritten_message that is clearer and more concise while keeping my voice encouraging and professional in first-person. Enforce all of the following constraints so the results are verifiable:
- Brevity: rewritten_message must be at least 10% shorter than the original (by character count), for every channel.
- Numeric integrity: preserve all numeric facts exactly. Every number, percentage, or currency amount present in the original (e.g., sequences of digits, optional commas/decimals, and optional leading "$" or trailing "%") must appear unchanged somewhere in rewritten_message. Do not introduce any new numbers that are not in the original.
- Channel length caps:
  - channel=="x": rewritten_message length <= 280 characters.
  - channel=="linkedin": rewritten_message length <= 700 characters.
  - channel=="newsletter": produce a single-sentence summary of the main point <= 200 characters.
- Tags handling: for channel in {"x","linkedin"}, append up to the first two tags from the input as hashtags at the end of rewritten_message (e.g., "#realestate #cashflow"). For channel=="newsletter", omit tags.

Outputs (write structured files locally):
1) For each processed input file inbox/<name>.jsonl, write cleaned posts to out/cleaned/<name>.jsonl. Each output line must be a JSON object with at least these fields: id, channel, original_length_chars, rewritten_length_chars, reduction_percent, rewritten_message, tags_used (array of hashtags appended, or empty), valid (boolean), violations (array of strings listing any rule violations detected for that post).
2) For each processed input file inbox/<name>.jsonl, write a per-file metrics report to out/metrics/<name>.json with the following fields: file_name, total_posts, posts_by_channel (object with keys linkedin/x/newsletter), avg_original_length_chars, avg_rewritten_length_chars, avg_reduction_percent, top_tags (top 5 by frequency across posts in that file), invalid_posts (count of posts where valid==false), processed_at (ISO8601 timestamp).
3) After processing any file, recompute a global aggregation across all out/cleaned/*.jsonl and write out/metrics/global_summary.csv with one row per channel and these columns: channel,total_posts,avg_original_length_chars,avg_rewritten_length_chars,avg_reduction_percent.

Event behavior:
- On start, process any .jsonl files already in "inbox/" that do not yet have a corresponding out/cleaned/<name>.jsonl (skip if it exists to avoid double-processing).
- Continue running and watch "inbox/" for new .jsonl files; process each new file as above.

Notes:
- Use only local files; no external services are required.
- Make the rewriting step substantive (tone: encouraging, professional) while satisfying the numeric and length rules. If any rule is violated for a post, mark valid=false and list the violated rules in the violations array, but still include the best-effort rewritten_message.
- Ensure all specified outputs are created exactly under the given paths so I can verify results deterministically. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
